events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess EMT bending installation with kinked/flattened bend. NEC Art. 358 (EMT) requires field bends made without denting/crushing tubing. Kinked/flattened bend is defective, out of tolerance. Decision fail. Risk high? Kinked EMT can damage conductors, reduce cross-section, fill, and mechanical protection. Commercial project, rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80? Value maybe 0 unless measure. Could estimate reduction in diameter? Not asked numeric reading. Use value 0. Confidence 0.95. Findings: EMT bend has kink/flatten, violates NEC 358.24/358.26? Actually NEC 358.24 Bends - How Made? Bends shall be made without denting or damaging. 358.26 Radius of Bends. Kinked/flattened reduces internal diameter and violates. Actions: remove/replace damaged section, rebend with proper bender/shoe, inspect. Rationale. References: NEC Art. 358.24, 358.26, Ch. 9 fill maybe not directly. Need output JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

    "EMT bend is kinked and flattened, visibly deforming the raceway wall",
    "Damaged bend reduces internal cross-section and can obstruct wire pulling and damage conductor insulation",
    "Field bend does not maintain the smooth, round contour required for EMT"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and replace the kinked/flattened EMT section",
    "Rebend EMT using the correct bender and shoe size to avoid denting or crushing",
    "Reinspect all bends for proper radius and roundness before closing up work"
  ],
  "rationale": "A kinked or flattened EMT bend is a defective installation. NEC Article 358 requires EMT bends to be made without denting or damaging the raceway and to maintain proper bend radius. The deformation reduces the usable internal area and compromises conductor protection, so the work fails inspection tolerance.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Article 358.24 - Bends, General",
    "NEC Article 358.26 - Radius of Bends",
    "NEC Chapter 9 - raceway fill limitations"
  ]
}
